report_name: Fake Interpol 'Cybercrime Investigation' Emails Deliver Custom Ransomware Globally

threat_actors.name: Interpol Impersonation Actor
threat_actors.description: An unsophisticated threat actor or group conducting a global phishing campaign impersonating Interpol to deliver custom ransomware to small businesses. The actor uses Tox for ransom negotiation and their ransomware contains a flaw where the decryption key is embedded in the executable, suggesting an amateur level of capability.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion of small and medium-sized businesses through ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Used social engineering in emails impersonating Interpol to trick victims into clicking a link to a Proton Drive archive containing the ransomware payload.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Victims were tricked into executing the ransomware payload, which was disguised as a video file within a downloaded password-protected archive.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The primary impact of the attack, where the custom ransomware encrypts files on the victim's system to demand a ransom.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware payload was delivered within a password-protected archive to evade automated scanning by email security gateways.

attack_patterns.name: Masquerading
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware executable was disguised as a video file named 'video_evidence.exe' to trick the user into running it.

attack_patterns.name: Impersonation
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor impersonated the 'Cybercrime Investigation Unit' at Interpol to create a sense of authority and urgency in their phishing emails.

malware.name: Interpol Impersonation Ransomware
malware.description: A custom, unsophisticated ransomware payload disguised as a video file. It encrypts victim files but contains a flawed implementation where the decryption key is embedded within the executable itself, allowing for potential recovery without payment.

tools.name: Proton Drive
tools.description: Legitimate cloud storage service used to host the password-protected archive containing the ransomware payload.

tools.name: Tox
tools.description: Encrypted P2P messaging application used as the communication channel for ransom negotiation.

campaigns.name: Interpol Impersonation Phishing Campaign
campaigns.description: A global phishing campaign observed in July 2026 targeting small businesses by impersonating Interpol. The campaign delivers custom ransomware via links to Proton Drive.

identities.name: Interpol
identities.description: International criminal police organization whose 'Cybercrime Investigation Unit' was impersonated by threat actors to lend legitimacy to a global phishing campaign.

identities.name: Small and Medium-sized Businesses
identities.description: The primary target demographic of the Interpol impersonation ransomware campaign, across various sectors including technology, finance, and legal services.

infrastructure.name: Ransomware Payload Hosting
infrastructure.description: Legitimate Proton Drive cloud storage was used to host the password-protected archive containing the ransomware.

infrastructure.name: Ransom Negotiation Channel
infrastructure.description: The Tox P2P network was used for anonymous ransom negotiation between the attackers and victims.

indicators.name: Interpol Impersonation Campaign Network Activity
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Interpol Impersonation Campaign Related DNS Queries
id: 7e5d9f8a-1b2c-4d5e-8f9a-0b1c2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to domains associated with the Interpol impersonation ransomware campaign. Proton Drive was used for payload delivery and Tox for negotiation. High volume of alerts may indicate malicious activity, but both services are legitimate.
references:
  - https://www.bitdefender.com/en-us/blog/hotforsecurity/fake-interpol-emails-serve-ransomware
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.initial_access
level: low
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of Proton Drive or Tox.
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith:
      - 'drive.proton.me'
      - 'tox.chat'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to domains used in the Interpol impersonation campaign. While both services are legitimate, an unusual increase in traffic, especially from non-standard processes, could indicate compromise.

indicators.name: Interpol Impersonation Ransomware Filenames
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Interpol Impersonation Ransomware Payload
id: 8f9a0b1c-2d3e-4f5a-b1c2-d3e4f5a6b7c8
status: experimental
description: Detects files with names used in the Interpol impersonation ransomware campaign.
references:
  - https://www.bitdefender.com/en-us/blog/hotforsecurity/fake-interpol-emails-serve-ransomware
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-02
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\evidence.zip'
      - '\video_evidence.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of files with names matching those used by the Interpol impersonation ransomware payload.
